Should a king observe this vow (or rule) established by Upari-

experience any trouble from his enemies.

Chapter XLIV—Lustration Ceremony
[An important item in the worship of Hindu Gods in temples
as well as homes is the waving of different kinds of lights before the
Deities. This is called snrfcf in the vernaculars and fftorr in Sanskrit.
Originally it consisted of purificatory rituals. This kind of worship
is also offered to kings, warriors, heroes, bridal couple etc. Even
royal emblems, horses etc. were recipients of this honour in ancient
times. It is done in the month of Karttika in the autumnal season
before kings undertook military expeditions. Actually it is a purifi-
